upcoding.description: Billing for more expensive services than provided
indicators: systematic shift to higher-paying procedure codes
impossible service volumes per provider per day

duplicate_claims.description: Submitting the same claim multiple times
indicators: identical claim fields within time window

unbundling.description: Separately billing bundled services for higher reimbursement
indicators: multiple related procedures billed separately on same day
component codes billed instead of comprehensive code
unusually high number of line items per encounter

identity_theft.description: Using stolen patient identities for fraudulent claims
indicators: sudden change in claim frequency or types
abnormal referral concentration between specific providers
referral patterns inconsistent with geographic proximity

theoretical_framework.TAM: Davis (1989) - Perceived usefulness and ease of use drive adoption
